title: Seseli foliosum
taxonomy: Seseli foliosum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It belongs to the genus Seseli, which consists of annual and perennial herbs. The plant is native to the Mediterranean region, and its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history. Within the family Apiaceae, Seseli foliosum is further grouped with other members of the subfamily Apioideae. The classification of Seseli foliosum provides insight into its relationships with other plants and its place within the plant kingdom.

intro: Seseli foliosum, also known as the leafy seseli, is a perennial plant species within the carrot family (Apiaceae). The leafy seseli has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been reported to aid in reducing fever, pain, and inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents of Seseli foliosum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Leafy seseli is commonly used in traditional medicine to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and digestive issues.
